title: Welcome to Entropic
description: A guided tour of the Entropic codebase — a local-first, cross-platform desktop AI workspace built with Tauri and React that runs OpenClaw inside an isolated, hardened container runtime.

steps.title: The Big Picture
steps.description: Entropic is a desktop AI workspace that runs OpenClaw in an isolated Docker container rather than executing commands directly on your machine. The codebase follows a layered architecture: a React/TypeScript frontend communicates through Tauri's IPC bridge to a Rust backend, which orchestrates a containerized OpenClaw runtime. Key top-level directories include src/ (frontend), src-tauri/ (backend), openclaw-runtime/ (container definition), and scripts/ (build tooling).

steps.title: Frontend Pages
steps.description: The React frontend lives in src/ and is organized around 14 page components. Chat.tsx is the main conversation interface, Dashboard.tsx is the home screen, Tasks.tsx and Jobs.tsx power the task board system, Settings.tsx handles configuration, Store.tsx is the plugin marketplace, and Channels.tsx manages integrations like WhatsApp and Telegram. The app uses React Router, Tailwind CSS, and Vite as the bundler.

steps.title: Frontend Core Libraries
steps.description: The src/lib/ directory contains 17 utility modules that power the frontend. gateway.ts is the WebSocket client for real-time OpenClaw communication. auth.ts handles OAuth flows and API key management. taskBoard.ts manages the task/job data model. vault.ts provides encrypted credential storage. integrations.ts handles plugin lifecycle. buildProfile.ts controls the two build modes: 'local' (bring-your-own-key) and 'managed' (hosted features with Supabase auth).

steps.title: Rust Backend (Tauri)
steps.description: The src-tauri/ directory contains the Rust backend. commands.rs (~15,500 lines) defines 150+ Tauri command endpoints covering runtime management, gateway control, authentication, browser automation, terminal access, skill scanning, and workspace file operations. runtime.rs (~4,100 lines) abstracts platform differences across macOS (Colima), Linux (Docker), and Windows (WSL). lib.rs initializes the app with encrypted storage (Stronghold), auto-updater, and event handlers.

steps.title: Sandboxed OpenClaw Runtime
steps.description: The openclaw-runtime/ directory defines the isolated container where OpenClaw actually runs. The Dockerfile builds a hardened environment with a non-root user, read-only root filesystem, Node.js 22, Python 3, and Patchright Chromium for browser automation. entrypoint.sh orchestrates skill installation and gateway startup. The browser-service/ subdirectory provides a sidecar for web automation with headless rendering support.

steps.title: Build & Deployment Scripts
steps.description: The scripts/ directory contains 30+ shell scripts for building, bundling, and deploying Entropic across platforms. dev-runtime.sh manages the local Colima VM for development. build-openclaw-runtime.sh builds the container image. bundle-runtime.sh packages the runtime for distribution. create-macos-dmg.sh and sign-notarize-macos.sh handle macOS packaging and code signing. The .github/workflows/ directory has CI/CD pipelines for releases across macOS, Linux, and Windows.

steps.title: Configuration & Build Profiles
steps.description: Entropic supports two build profiles: 'local' (default, contributor-friendly, bring-your-own API keys) and 'managed' (official releases with Supabase auth, billing, and auto-updates). Key config files include tauri.conf.json for production Tauri settings, vite.config.ts for the frontend bundler, and Cargo.toml for Rust dependencies. The docs/ directory contains architecture specs, roadmaps, and security documentation.

steps.title: Start Exploring
steps.description: You now have an overview of Entropic's architecture: React frontend pages and libraries in src/, Rust backend commands and runtime management in src-tauri/, the sandboxed OpenClaw container in openclaw-runtime/, and build tooling in scripts/. The codebase emphasizes security through isolation, cross-platform support, and a clean separation between the desktop shell and the AI runtime. Dive into any area that interests you!
